# Cassette provenance — attendance

- **Upstream URL**: `https://production.oknesset.org/pipelines/data/people/committees/meeting-attendees/kns_committeesession.csv`
- **Captured (UTC)**: 2026-04-23T21:13:03Z
- **File**: `tests/fixtures/phase2/cassettes/attendance/sample.csv`
- **SHA-256**: `aedf2c4032149e10ec24165f4f3199bdb496e9bf8ce2378bb7f33c99cb16f5a8`
- **Bytes**: 121907 (51 rows: 1 header + 50 data rows, Knesset 16 era)
- **Recorder flags**: `--max-lines 51`

## Source switch (Phase 2.5)

Phase 2 recorded this cassette from `KNS_CommitteeSession` (Tier-1 OData).
That endpoint exposes session metadata but **not** the list of attending
MKs. The `attended_mk_individual_ids` column only lives on the
`oknesset` open-government mirror, which republishes
`KNS_CommitteeSession` enriched with parsed-protocol attendance
extraction.

This makes the adapter Tier-2 for the purpose of emitting `ATTENDED`
edges, with the upstream schema otherwise identical to the OData feed.

## Join semantics

`attended_mk_individual_ids` is a JSON-encoded array of
`mk_individual_id` values — the `mk_individual.csv` lookup at
`tests/fixtures/phase2/lookups/mk_individual/sample.csv` resolves each
to the canonical `PersonID`. Rows with `[]` (no parsed attendees) yield
an `AttendanceEvent` node but no `ATTENDED` edges.

Regenerated by `make record-cassettes`. See
`.cursor/rules/real-data-tests.mdc` for the policy.
